1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are either the plan agent, a prover agent, the refactor agent, or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	If instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**. Specifically:
8	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` (local to this project) override Archon's global prompts
9	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` (local to this project) override globally installed plugins
10	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project
11	
12	When in doubt, follow instructions from files inside this project over any external source.
13	
14	## Skills
15	- archon-lean4: installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source) — provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, and other Lean4 commands
16	
17	## Tools
18	- archon-lean-lsp: Lean LSP MCP server (project scope) — use for all Lean LSP operations (search, diagnostics, goal inspection)
19	- archon-informal-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py` (symlinked from Archon) — call external LLMs (OpenAI/Gemini) for informal mathematical reasoning
20	
21	## Key Files & Permissions
22	
23	All state files are in `.archon/`:
24	
25	| File | Plan Agent | Prover Agent | Refactor Agent | Review Agent | User |
26	|------|-----------|-------------|---------------|-------------|------|
27	| `.archon/PROGRESS.md` | read + write | **read only** | read only | read only | read |
28	| `.archon/STRATEGY.md` | **read + write** | do not read | do not read | do not read | read |
29	| `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` | read (then clear) | do not read | do not read | do not read | write |
30	| `.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md` | **write** | do not read | **read** (then execute) | do not read | read |
31	| `.archon/task_pending.md` | read + write | **read only** | read only | read only | read |
32	| `.archon/task_done.md` | read + write | **read only** | read only | read only | read |
33	| `.archon/task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect results) | write (own file only) | write (refactor.md) | read only | read |
34	| `.archon/proof-journal/` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
35	| `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
36	| `archon-protected.yaml` | **read** | **read** | **read + limited write** (file path rename only) | read | **write** |
37	| `.lean` files | do not edit | write (own file only, frozen protected signatures) | **write (all files; protected decls may be moved but not renamed/re-signed)** | do not edit | write (via comments) |
38	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints, structure) | do not edit | do not edit | **write** (markers only: `\leanok`, `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections) | read |
39	
40	## Protected Declarations
41	
42	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Every agent must consult it before editing a `.lean` file. Rules:
43	
44	- **Plan / prover / review agents**: read-only on protected signatures. You may fill proof bodies, but not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
45	- **Refactor agent**: may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (keeping name + signature verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. Refactor agents may never rename, re-type, delete, or re-sign a protected declaration.
46	- The `archon-protected.yaml` file can only be edited by the user, no declaration can be added or removed by any agent. The only allowed modification is updating the file path of an existing protected declaration when its location was changed. 
47	
48	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
49	
50	The blueprint uses two active markers.
51	
52	- `\leanok` — inside a statement block when the declaration is formalized with at least a `sorry`; inside a proof block when the proof is fully closed with no `sorry`.
53	- `\mathlibok` — inside a statement block when the declaration already exists in Mathlib and the Archon side is a re-export/alias; no Archon proof obligation remains.
54	- No marker — the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
55	
56	**The review agent is the sole writer of these markers.** Prover agents (autoformalize / prover / polish) never touch the blueprint chapters — they describe their outcome in `task_results/<file>.md` and the review agent verifies and marks accordingly. The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints, but never markers.
57	
58	## User Interaction
59	
60	Users provide hints in two places:
61	
62	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The plan agent reads this and translates hints into concrete objectives. Provers never read this file.
63	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
64	
65	## Agent Roles
66	
67	### Plan Agent
68	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for your full instructions
69	- Read `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` — incorporate hints, then clear them after acting
70	- Read `.archon/task_results/` — collect prover results, then update `task_pending.md` and `task_done.md`
71	- Read `.archon/task_results/refactor.md` - if refactor agent has run, read their report and adjust your plans accordingly
72	- Write `.archon/PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round
73	- Write `.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md` when structural changes are needed
74	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself
75	
76	### Prover Agent
77	- Read `.archon/PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
78	- Read the stage-specific prompt from `.archon/prompts/`:
79	  - autoformalize → `.archon/prompts/prover-autoformalize.md`
80	  - prover → `.archon/prompts/prover-prover.md`
81	  - polish → `.archon/prompts/prover-polish.md`
82	- Write results to `.archon/task_results/<your_file>.md`
83	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — **never edit another agent's file**
84	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your `.lean` file for file-specific hints
85	- **Do NOT edit blueprint chapters.** Marker updates are the review agent's responsibility. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker.
86	
87	### Refactor Agent
88	- Read `.archon/prompts/refactor.md` for your full instructions
89	- Read `.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md` for the plan agent's directive
90	- Modify any `.lean` file: definitions, signatures, types, imports
91	- Keep all files compiling (insert `sorry` at broken proof sites)
92	- Do NOT fill proofs — that's the prover's job
93	- Write results to `.archon/task_results/refactor.md`
94	
95	### Review Agent
96	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for your full instructions
97	- Read `.archon/proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data
98	- Write session journal to `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (summary.md, milestones.jsonl, recommendations.md)
99	- Update `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` with overall progress
100	- **Update blueprint markers** (`\leanok`, `\mathlibok`) in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` based on verified compilation and the provers' task results
101	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify PROGRESS.md

1	# Prover — Prover Stage
2	
3	You are the prover agent in the proving stage. Your job: fill `sorry` placeholders with complete proofs.
4	
5	## Workflow
6	
7	1. Read `PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
8	2. Read `task_pending.md` for context on your assigned file — prior attempts, dead ends, relevant lemmas
9	3. Check your `.lean` file for `/- USER: ... -/` comments — these are file-specific hints from the user
10	4. Before writing Lean code, you **MUST** consult the relevant blueprint chapter. Blueprints contain mathematical proof sketches; your formal proof must align with them. When stuck, re-reading the blueprint is often the fastest path forward.
11	5. Replace `sorry` with Lean proofs, pushing as far as possible
12	6. **Always save partial progress in the code.** If you cannot fully prove a sorry, replace it with your best attempt — commented-out proof steps, helper lemmas, partial `by` blocks with remaining `sorry` at the stuck point. The file must still compile (use scoped `sorry` for the stuck parts), but your work must be visible in the code for the next agent to continue from. NEVER revert to the original bare `sorry` — that wastes all your work.
13	7. Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md` — what you tried, what worked, what's stuck, next steps
14	
15	**Write permissions**: You may only write to your assigned `.lean` file(s) and your `task_results/<file>.md`. Do NOT edit `PROGRESS.md`, `task_pending.md`, `task_done.md`, or other agents' files.
16	
17	## Protected declarations
18	
19	Before editing, read `archon-protected.yaml` at the project root. Declarations listed for your file have **frozen signatures** — you may fill their proof bodies but must not rename them, change their types, reorder their arguments, or weaken their hypotheses. Only the mathematician is allowed to edit protected signatures. 
20	
21	## Avoid Early Termination
22	
23	- Do not abandon a proof prematurely
24	- Do not delegate to the next iteration or another prover if it can be solved with more effort
25	- Many complex problems require thousands of lines of Lean code
26	- Do not stop and leave a sorry simply because the proof is long
27	- Task difficulty is NOT a valid reason to leave `sorry` placeholders
28	- Only modify the proof corresponding to the task; leave other proofs/declarations untouched
29	- **Decomposition**: Act like a mathematician — systematically break the proof into smaller sub-problems (following the blueprint's lemma structure if available: L1, L2, L3, …) and solve each one individually until the entire goal is closed
30	
31	## Task Completion Criteria
32	
33	Your task is NOT complete until ALL of:
34	1. Every `sorry` has been replaced with a complete proof
35	2. Zero axioms introduced
36	3. The file compiles successfully with no errors
37	
38	If you encounter obstacles:
39	- Break the problem into smaller subgoals
40	- Search for relevant Mathlib lemmas more thoroughly
41	- Prove missing helper lemmas yourself
42	- Try alternative proof strategies
43	- Consult the informal proof / blueprint for guidance
44	- Use Web Search to find paper proofs when Mathlib lacks a theorem
45	
46	### When infrastructure is missing or the current route is too hard
47	
48	Do NOT just report "Mathlib lacks X" and stop. Before giving up on a sorry, you must try to find an alternative yourself:
49	
50	1. **Use the informal agent** (`.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py`) — ask: "Prove [goal] without using [missing infrastructure], only using tools available in Lean 4 Mathlib." Even an imperfect sketch is valuable.
51	2. **Try the alternative** — if the informal agent gives you a route, attempt to formalize it.
52	3. **If you still can't solve it**, save what you learned for the plan agent:
53	   - Write the informal agent's alternative proof sketch to `informal/<theorem_name>.md`
54	   - In your `task_results/<file>.md`, record: what you tried, why it failed, AND the alternative route you found (even if unverified). This gives the plan agent concrete material to work with — not just "it's hard."
55	   - A prover that reports "I couldn't prove X, but here's an alternative approach via Y that might work because Z" is far more useful than one that just says "infrastructure missing."
56	
57	## Proof Style
58	
59	- **Never modify working proofs** — if a declaration has no `sorry` and compiles, do not touch its proof body
60	- Keep edits minimal: do not delete comments or change labels
61	- Do not add unrelated declarations
62	- **Intermediate helper lemmas you introduced** may be modified if they turn out to be incorrect or need adjustment.
63	- Add concise, informative comments above helper lemmas to make later reuse easy
64	
65	## Search Protocol
66	
67	Follow the search tool priority and query guidance in the lean4 skill reference (`references/lean-lsp-tools-api.md`). Key points:
68	
69	1. `lean_local_search` first
70	2. `lean_leansearch` for semantic search — **describe the mathematical content**, not just the name
71	3. `lean_loogle` for simple type patterns only
72	4. Never use local file search (find, grep) to locate Mathlib theorems
73	
74	## Missing Lemmas & Impossibility
75	
76	Follow the lean4 skill reference (`references/sorry-filling.md`) for:
77	- **When Mathlib lacks a theorem**: bypass or implement yourself. Web Search for published papers. Never leave a `sorry` just because Mathlib doesn't have it.
78	- **Distinguish impossibility from difficulty**: technical difficulty → keep trying. Mathematical impossibility → immediately backtrack and document why.
79	
80	## Logging
81	
82	Write your results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`. Use the file name from your assigned `.lean` file (e.g., if you own `Algebra/WLocal.lean`, write to `task_results/Algebra_WLocal.lean.md`).
83	
84	**Format:**
85	
86	```markdown
87	# Algebra/WLocal.lean
88	
89	## wLocal_iff (line 45)
90	### Attempt 1
91	- **Approach:** Direct case split on maximal ideals
92	- **Result:** FAILED — needed IsLocalRing instance not available
93	- **Dead end:** Do not try direct case split without IsLocalRing
94	
95	### Attempt 2
96	- **Approach:** Use Stacks 0A31, characterize via bijection on spectra
97	- **Result:** RESOLVED
98	- **Key insight:** Mathlib's PrimeSpectrum.comap_injective bridges the gap
99	
100	## helper_bijective (line 78)
101	### Attempt 1
102	- **Approach:** Function.Bijective.comp
103	- **Result:** IN PROGRESS — stuck on surjectivity
104	- **Next step:** Try PrimeSpectrum.range_comap_of_surjective
105	- **Relevant lemmas found:** PrimeSpectrum.comap_surjective
106	```
107	
108	**Rules:**
109	1. One section per theorem/lemma in your file
110	2. Each attempt records: approach, result (RESOLVED / FAILED / IN PROGRESS), dead-end warnings or next steps
111	3. Log negative search results (e.g., "Searched 'projective module infinite rank' — nothing in Mathlib")
112	4. The plan agent collects these files and merges them into `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`
113	
114	**Read-only references:** Read `task_pending.md` for prior context on your file. Read `task_done.md` if the current problem resembles a completed one. Do not write to either.
115	
116	## Summary Pipeline
117	
118	1. Read `task_pending.md` and `task_done.md` for context from prior sessions
119	2. Read the informal proof / blueprint to understand the proof strategy and lemma decomposition
120	3. Introduce helper lemmas (matching the blueprint's structure) in the `.lean` file
121	4. Replace `sorry` placeholders with complete proofs, ensuring the file compiles without errors
122	5. Do not modify protected declarations listed in `archon-protected.yaml`.
123	6. Use Mathlib theorems when possible. Use Web Search when Mathlib lacks referenced results
124	7. Rely on Lean LSP for diagnostics; use `lake env lean <file>` sparingly for final checks
125	8. Log all explorations in `task_results/<your_file>.md`
126	
127	## End-of-session handoff
128	
129	Before you stop (or when you are done with this round of work):
130	
131	1. Write to `task_results/<your_file>.md` with:
132	   - Current result (IN PROGRESS / FAILED) and what you tried
133	   - Any Mathlib lemmas you discovered that are relevant
134	   - Concrete next step for the next session
135	   - Dead-end warnings for approaches that won't work
136	2. Save all file changes (ensure compilation passes, using scoped `sorry` if needed)
137

1	# Project Progress
2	
3	## Current Stage
4	prover  (iter-004 prover round: close the 4 scaffold sorries laid by the iter-004 refactor)
5	
6	## Stages
7	- [x] init
8	- [x] autoformalize
9	- [ ] prover
10	- [ ] polish
11	
12	## Big-picture context
13	
14	This project formalizes the Jacobian of a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve over a field, following Christian Merten's challenge (`references/challenge.lean`). The nine declarations in `archon-protected.yaml` are the deliverables — their signatures are frozen.
15	
16	`STRATEGY.md` describes the multi-phase build-out path (Phases A–E). The 9 protected sorries continue to be blocked behind missing Mathlib infrastructure; the iter-004 refactor opens two parallel low-coupling helper tracks (Phase A steps 2–4 wiring; Phase C step 3 codomain change) that the upcoming prover round will close.
17	
18	## Iteration 004 — post-refactor verification (PASS)
19	
20	The iter-004 refactor (`task_results/refactor.md`) is verified by the plan agent:
21	
22	- **Compile**: `lake build` exits 0; `lean_diagnostic_messages` per-file confirms only the expected sorry warnings, no errors.
23	- **Sorry counts**: total 14 (was 10 pre-refactor; +4 new scaffold sites). Per file:
24	  - `Cohomology/StructureSheafAb.lean` (NEW) — 3 sorries (L34 `instHasSheafify_Opens_AddCommGrp`, L42 `instHasExt_Sheaf_Opens_AddCommGrp`, L54 `Scheme.toAbSheaf`).
25	  - `Picard/FunctorAb.lean` (NEW) — 1 sorry (L41 `Scheme.PicardFunctorAb`).
26	  - `Picard/Functor.lean` — 1 sorry (L185 `PicardFunctor.representable`, deferred indefinitely; not in scope of this prover round).
27	  - `Genus.lean`, `Jacobian.lean`, `AbelJacobi.lean` — 9 protected sorries (untouched).
28	- **Signatures**: all four scaffold signatures match the directive verbatim — namespaces (`AlgebraicGeometry.Cohomology` for the two instances, `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme` for the def and the `PicardFunctorAb`), universe annotations (`.{u}` on each category, `HasExt.{u+1}` on the sheaf category), and source category (`(Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))ᵒᵖ` for `PicardFunctorAb`).
29	- **Imports**: `AlgebraicJacobian.lean` umbrella now imports the two new files in dependency order. Side-effect of the refactor: the umbrella's existing imports were also reordered to follow the dependency tree. No declarations were renamed or moved.
30	- **Protected**: `archon-protected.yaml` unchanged. No protected declaration was edited.
31	- **Blueprint**: `Cohomology_StructureSheafAb.tex` and `Picard_FunctorAb.tex` already exist (written by this plan agent in iter-004 pre-refactor); both are wired into `content.tex`. The `\lean{...}` macros resolve to the four new declarations exactly. No marker changes are due — the prover has not yet run.
32	- **Axioms**: no new `axiom` declarations anywhere (only the 14 sorries).
33	- **Forbidden shortcuts**: none — the refactor laid scaffold only, no proofs were filled.
34	
35	The refactor is therefore accepted. Iteration 004 now advances to the prover round, with two parallel objectives (one per new file).
36	
37	## Current Objectives — iter-004 prover round
38	
39	Two parallel agents, one per new file. **Do not** assign any objective on the protected files (`Genus.lean`, `Jacobian.lean`, `AbelJacobi.lean`) or on `Picard/Functor.lean` (its representability sorry is deferred indefinitely; see "Known dead ends").
40	
41	### Objective 1 — `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/StructureSheafAb.lean` (Phase A steps 2–4 wiring)
42	
43	Close the **three** sorries in this file:
44	
45	- **L34** `AlgebraicGeometry.Cohomology.instHasSheafify_Opens_AddCommGrp` — `HasSheafify (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) AddCommGrpCat.{u}` for `X : TopCat.{u}`.
46	- **L42** `AlgebraicGeometry.Cohomology.instHasExt_Sheaf_Opens_AddCommGrp` — `HasExt.{u+1} (Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) AddCommGrpCat.{u})` for `X : TopCat.{u}`.
47	- **L54** `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.toAbSheaf` — for `C : Scheme.{u}`, the abelian-group sheaf on `Opens.grothendieckTopology C.toTopCat` obtained by composing `C.sheaf` with `forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat ⋙ forget₂ RingCat AddCommGrpCat`.
48	
49	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_StructureSheafAb.tex` (`thm:HasSheafify_Opens_AddCommGrp`, `thm:HasExt_Sheaf_Opens_AddCommGrp`, `def:Scheme_toAbSheaf`).
50	
51	**Plan-agent live Mathlib probe (iter-004) confirms each sorry is closeable:**
52	
53	1. **`instHasSheafify_Opens_AddCommGrp`** — expected closure: `inferInstance` after universe pinning. The probe ran
54	   ```
55	   example (X : TopCat.{u}) :
56	       HasSheafify (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) AddCommGrpCat.{u} := inferInstance
57	   ```
58	   and it succeeded. The instance is composed by Mathlib from `HasWeakSheafify.{…}` plus the preservation-of-finite-limits side condition; both are pre-existing for the `Opens` topology and `AddCommGrpCat`. Mirror the universe-pinning style of the iter-003 `instHasSheafCompose_…` closure (`AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/SheafCompose.lean`): annotate each category with `.{u}` so the implicit universe variables in the Mathlib-side instance unify on the nose.
59	2. **`instHasExt_Sheaf_Opens_AddCommGrp`** — expected closure: `CategoryTheory.HasExt.standard _`. The probe ran
60	   ```
61	   noncomputable example (X : TopCat.{u}) :
62	       HasExt.{u+1} (Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) AddCommGrpCat.{u}) :=
63	     HasExt.standard _
64	   ```
65	   and it succeeded. `HasExt.standard` produces a `HasExt` instance from any abelian category (the abelian instance on `Sheaf (Opens.gT X) AddCommGrpCat.{u}` is automatic via the iter-003 `HasSheafCompose` plus Objective 1's `HasSheafify`). The morphism universe of the sheaf category forces the annotation `HasExt.{u+1}`; do not change it. If the bare `HasExt.standard _` does not type-check on first try, look for the explicit anonymous-typeclass placeholder pattern (`HasExt.standard (C := …)`).
66	3. **`Scheme.toAbSheaf`** — expected closure: a one-line term obtained by composing the iter-003 `HasSheafCompose` instance with `C.sheaf`:
67	   ```
68	   noncomputable def toAbSheaf (C : Scheme.{u}) :
69	       Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.toTopCat) AddCommGrpCat.{u} :=
70	     (sheafCompose (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.toTopCat)
71	       (forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat ⋙ forget₂ RingCat AddCommGrpCat)).obj C.sheaf
72	   ```
73	   The functor `sheafCompose J F` is Mathlib's bundled "post-compose with `F`" on the sheaf category; its `obj` map applied to `C.sheaf : Sheaf (Opens.gT X) CommRingCat` produces the desired `Sheaf (Opens.gT X) AddCommGrpCat`. The `HasSheafCompose` instance on the forget composite is `instHasSheafCompose_forget_CommRing_AddCommGrp` from `Cohomology/SheafCompose.lean` (iter-003) — it should be picked up automatically by typeclass resolution; ensure the universe annotations on the two `forget₂` factors are consistent (`forget₂ CommRingCat.{u} RingCat.{u}` and `forget₂ RingCat.{u} AddCommGrpCat.{u}`, matching iter-003's instance).
74	
75	If any of the three closures requires more than typeclass plumbing — e.g.\ the probe-style closure does not type-check verbatim and an additional lemma is needed — do not bypass the issue with `decide`/`trivial`/`Discrete PUnit` shortcuts. Document the obstruction in `task_results/Cohomology_StructureSheafAb.lean.md` and let the next plan-agent round re-route. The plan agent's probe confirms the math is in Mathlib; only the Lean-side packaging may need a small adjustment (e.g.\ explicit instance arguments).
76	
77	**Forbidden shortcuts** for this objective: `:= sorry`-still, `:= ()`/`:= PUnit`/any vacuous-sheaf placeholder, `decide`, `trivial`, new `axiom` declarations. The construction must produce the actual Mathlib `HasSheafify` / `HasExt` / sheaf-composition output.
78	
79	**Reusable patterns from prior iterations:**
80	
81	- **Universe pinning** (iter-003): explicit `.{u}` on each category in a chain of `PreservesLimitsOfSize`-style instances. Apply to all three closures.
82	- **Snake_case lemma names** in `CategoryTheory.Limits.Preserves.Basic` (iter-003).
83	- **`Type u` morphisms wrapped in `TypeCat.Hom`** (iter-003) — relevant if an underlying-set lemma is invoked.
84	
85	After closing all three sorries, list any `\leanok` candidates in the task result so the review agent can update the blueprint markers.
86	
87	### Objective 2 — `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/FunctorAb.lean` (Phase C step 3 codomain change)
88	
89	Close the **single** sorry in this file:
90	
91	- **L41** `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicardFunctorAb` — for `C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))`, the contravariant functor `(Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))ᵒᵖ ⥤ AddCommGrpCat.{u}` whose underlying-set functor agrees on the nose with `PicardFunctor C` (Chapter `Picard_Functor.tex`).
92	
93	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_FunctorAb.tex` (`def:Pic_functorAb`).
94	
95	**Expected closure**: a concrete `Functor` constructor analogous to the iter-003 `PicardFunctor` (`AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/Functor.lean` L158–174), with the codomain wrapped in `AddCommGrpCat`:
96	
97	```
98	noncomputable def PicardFunctorAb
99	    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))) :
100	    (Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))ᵒᵖ ⥤ AddCommGrpCat.{u} where
101	  obj S := AddCommGrpCat.of
102	    (Pic (Limits.pullback C.hom S.unop.hom) ⧸
103	      (Pic.pullback (Limits.pullback.snd C.hom S.unop.hom)).range)
104	  map {_ _} f := AddCommGrpCat.ofHom (PicardFunctor.quotMap C f.unop)
105	  map_id _ := by
106	    -- reduce to PicardFunctor.quotMap_id
107	    ...
108	  map_comp f g := by
109	    -- reduce to PicardFunctor.quotMap_comp
110	    ...
111	```
112	
113	The helper lemmas are already in `Picard/Functor.lean`:
114	
115	- `PicardFunctor.quotMap : (Pic ... ⧸ ...) →* (Pic ... ⧸ ...)` is the underlying `MonoidHom`. `AddCommGrpCat.ofHom` should accept it directly (a commutative-group `MonoidHom` is also an `AddMonoidHom` under Mathlib's additive-multiplicative duality on `Pic`; if the bundled-instance idiom requires an explicit `MonoidHom.toAdditive`-style transport, the prover may need to either (a) keep `Pic` multiplicative and use an `AddCommGrpCat`-valued idiom that accepts a `MonoidHom`, or (b) reformulate the quotient as additive). The blueprint chapter explicitly endorses path (a) ("wrapped via `AddCommGrpCat.ofHom`"); confirm this lines up with Mathlib's bundled-category API before resorting to (b).
116	- `PicardFunctor.quotMap_id` (`@[simp]`) — reduces to `MonoidHom.id _`.
117	- `PicardFunctor.quotMap_comp` — `quotMap C (g ≫ f) = (quotMap C g).comp (quotMap C f)`.
118	
119	`map_id` and `map_comp` should reduce to these via `AddCommGrpCat.ofHom_id` / `AddCommGrpCat.ofHom_comp` (or whichever lemmas Mathlib `b80f227` ships under `AddCommGrpCat.ofHom`). Use `lean_local_search` / `lean_loogle` to find the exact lemma names if unsure.
120	
121	**Forbidden shortcuts** for this objective: `:= sorry`-still, `:= Discrete PUnit`/vacuous-functor placeholder, returning a constant `0` group, redefining `obj` to discard the quotient. The construction must reproduce the exact group `Pic (C ×_k S) / p_S^* Pic(S)` that `PicardFunctor C` already returns at the underlying-set level.
122	
123	**Reusable patterns from prior iterations:**
124	
125	- **`(Over (Spec k))ᵒᵖ` source category** (iter-003) — keep this verbatim; the source category was already chosen at iter-003 to match the iter-002 `Pic.pullback` API.
126	- **`fiberMap` / `quotMap` helpers** (iter-003) — already in scope via `import AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.Functor`. Use them directly; do not re-derive.
127	- **`AddCommGrpCat.ofHom` of a `MonoidHom`** — if Mathlib `b80f227` requires an `AddMonoidHom` instead, search for `AddCommGrpCat.ofHom`'s actual signature first and adapt.
128	
129	After closing the sorry, list `\leanok` candidates in the task result for the review agent.
130	
131	### Difficulty estimate / parallelism
132	
133	Objective 1 is "three pieces of typeclass plumbing" — moderate, ~20 LOC including imports and namespace boilerplate; the bulk is universe-pinning fights.
134	Objective 2 is "one concrete `Functor` constructor on top of two existing helper lemmas" — easy, ~25 LOC. The `map_id` / `map_comp` proofs are one-liners after the `obj` / `map` shape is right.
135	
136	Both are independent: `FunctorAb.lean` does not import `StructureSheafAb.lean`, and vice-versa. The two prover agents run in parallel.
137	
138	## Anticipated post-prover state
139	
140	If both objectives close honestly:
141	
142	- Sorry count: 14 → 10 (back to the 10 pre-refactor: 9 protected + 1 deferred `representable`).
143	- Blueprint markers: `\leanok` on the four new declaration statements; `\leanok` on the proofs of `thm:HasSheafify_Opens_AddCommGrp`, `thm:HasExt_Sheaf_Opens_AddCommGrp`; `\leanok` on `def:Scheme_toAbSheaf` (statement only — it's a definition); `\leanok` on `def:Pic_functorAb` (statement only). The review agent updates these.
144	- After this round, **the abelian-group level of `H^n(C, O_C)` is well-defined** as `(Scheme.toAbSheaf C).H n` (using Mathlib's `Sheaf.H` API + the new `HasExt` instance). The remaining work to an honest `genus` is Phase A step 5 (`Module k` structure on `H¹`) and step 6 (Serre finiteness); both are deferred to iter-005+.
145	
146	If a prover hits an obstruction not foreseen by the plan-agent probe — e.g.\ Mathlib's bundled-category API requires a different idiom for `AddCommGrpCat.ofHom` of a `MonoidHom`, or the universe-pinning style does not transfer cleanly from the iter-003 `HasSheafCompose` closure — they should document it in `task_results/<file>.lean.md` and stop. The plan agent will re-route in the next iteration.
147	
148	## Reusable proof patterns (for the upcoming prover briefings)
149	
150	1. **Universe pinning when chaining `PreservesLimitsOfSize` / `HasSheafify` instances** (iter-003) — explicit `.{u}` annotations on each category. Apply to all three Objective-1 closures.
151	2. **Snake_case lemma names in `CategoryTheory.Limits.Preserves.Basic`** (iter-003).
152	3. **`Type u` morphisms wrapped in `TypeCat.Hom`** (iter-003) — relevant if an underlying-set lemma is invoked.
153	4. **`pullback.hom_ext` + simp lemmas for fiber-product functoriality** (iter-003).
154	5. **`QuotientGroup.eq_one_iff` for descent through `QuotientGroup.lift`** (iter-003) — already inside `quotMap`; the prover only consumes it for Objective 2.
155	6. **Source-category `(Over (Spec k))ᵒᵖ` for relative functors** (iter-003) — `PicardFunctorAb` keeps the same source as `PicardFunctor`.
156	7. **First-approximation pattern** (iter-002) — the `LineBundle` first-approximation flows through `PicardFunctor` to `PicardFunctorAb`; the codomain change is purely typeclass.
157	8. **`HasExt.standard` for an abelian sheaf category** (new, iter-004 probe) — produces `HasExt` from any abelian category; the abelian instance comes from `HasSheafify`.
158	9. **`sheafCompose J F` to post-compose a sheaf with a functor on the codomain** (new, iter-004) — Mathlib bundled functor on the sheaf category; takes a `HasSheafCompose J F` instance.
159	
160	## Known dead ends (do not retry)
161	
162	- The 9 protected sorries (`Genus.lean` × 1, `Jacobian.lean` × 5, `AbelJacobi.lean` × 3) — all blocked behind upstream Mathlib infrastructure (Phase A step 5/6 for `genus`; Phase C representability for `Jacobian`; Phase C + Phase E for `AbelJacobi`). Do not assign as direct prover objectives.
163	- `PicardFunctor.representable` (`Picard/Functor.lean` L185) — closing on the global-sections-approximate `LineBundle` is a forbidden shortcut. Honest closure requires `LineBundle` refinement (gated on `MonoidalCategory X.Modules`) plus the FGA argument. Multi-iteration; do not retry.
164	- `LineBundle` direct refinement — gated on `MonoidalCategory X.Modules` (absent in `b80f227`). Multi-iteration; do not issue as a primary objective.
165	- `HasWeakSheafify Scheme.etaleTopology AddCommGrpCat` — genuine Mathlib gap (verified iter-004 `lean_run_code` probe). Multi-iteration; not in scope of this prover round.
166	- Vacuous-functor closures (`Discrete PUnit`, etc.) — destroy the downstream Picard chain; rejected on sight.
167	- Vacuous-sheaf closures for the Phase A wiring (`PUnit.{u+1}`-valued sheaves, etc.) — destroy the downstream cohomology chain; rejected on sight.
168	- New `axiom` declarations — forbidden by plan-agent rules.
169	- Symmetric topological-equality form of rigidity (`∀ x ∈ U, g₁.left.base x = g₂.left.base x ⇒ g₁ = g₂`) — false in characteristic `p` (Frobenius). Resolved iter-002 with the scheme-level form.
170	- `MonoidalCategory X.Modules` is absent in `b80f227` (verified iter-002); building it is multi-iteration, not in scope.
171	
172	## Output protocol
173	
174	Each prover writes to `task_results/<file>.lean.md`. Required content:
175	
176	- which sorries were closed (line numbers and declaration names);
177	- which sorries (if any) were left open and why (with a clear obstruction summary the plan agent can act on next iteration);
178	- the actual closure body (fenced code block) for each closed sorry, so the plan agent can verify quickly without re-reading the whole file;
179	- any minor name adjustments required by Mathlib `b80f227` (analogous to iter-003's `Opens.grothendieckTopology` rename or the `(Over (Spec k))ᵒᵖ` source-category reshape);
180	- whether any `axiom` declarations were considered (the answer must be "no");
181	- proposed `\leanok` markers (on which `thm:…` or `def:…` blocks).
182	
183	The plan agent will independently verify (sorry count + `lean_diagnostic_messages` + axiom check via `lean_verify` + blueprint consistency via `leanblueprint checkdecls`) before declaring the round accepted.
184	
185	## Related state files
186	
187	- `STRATEGY.md` — five-phase build-out plan; revision log records iter-001 through iter-004 changes. Iter-004 entry records the substantive Phase A revision (steps 2–3 inferable from Mathlib).
188	- `task_pending.md` — consolidated gap report and helper-target index. Track B steps 2–4 + Track C step 3 codomain change marked as "scaffolded; prover round in flight".
189	- `task_done.md` — iter-002 closures (`eq_of_eqOnOpen`, `LineBundle`, `instCommGroupLineBundle`, `Pic.pullback`) and iter-003 closures (`instHasSheafCompose_…`, `PicardFunctor` definition).

192	- `PROJECT_STATUS.md` — written by the iter-003 review agent (session 3). Will be updated by the review agent after this prover round.

6	import Mathlib
7	
8	/-!
9	# Sheaf condition along the structure-sheaf forget composite
10	
11	Phase A step 1 (per `STRATEGY.md`): the `HasSheafCompose` instance for the
12	forget composite `CommRingCat ⥤ RingCat ⥤ AddCommGrpCat` on the Grothendieck
13	topology of opens of an arbitrary topological space. This is the gateway to
14	computing `H¹(C, O_C)` as an abelian group (and ultimately as a `k`-vector
15	space for the genus).
16	
17	See `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_SheafCompose.tex`.
18	
19	## Status (iteration 004 — refactor scaffold)
20	
21	This file is a scaffold. The single instance below is `sorry`. The iter-005
22	prover is responsible for filling it (Phase A step 1 of `STRATEGY.md`).
23	-/
24	
25	set_option autoImplicit false
26	
27	universe u
28	
29	open CategoryTheory Limits
30	
31	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Cohomology
32	
33	/-- The forget composite `CommRingCat ⥤ RingCat ⥤ AddCommGrpCat` preserves the
34	sheaf condition on the Grothendieck topology of opens of any topological space.
35	Equivalently: a sheaf of commutative rings on a topological space is in
36	particular a sheaf of abelian groups (post-composing with the forgetful is
37	sound). -/
38	instance instHasSheafCompose_forget_CommRing_AddCommGrp (X : TopCat.{u}) :
39	    (Opens.grothendieckTopology X).HasSheafCompose
40	      (CategoryTheory.forget₂ CommRingCat.{u} RingCat.{u} ⋙
41	       CategoryTheory.forget₂ RingCat.{u} AddCommGrpCat.{u}) :=
42	  haveI : PreservesLimitsOfSize.{u, u}
43	      (CategoryTheory.forget₂ CommRingCat.{u} RingCat.{u} ⋙
44	        CategoryTheory.forget₂ RingCat.{u} AddCommGrpCat.{u}) :=
45	    Limits.comp_preservesLimits _ _
46	  CategoryTheory.hasSheafCompose_of_preservesLimitsOfSize _
47	
48	end AlgebraicGeometry.Cohomology

# AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/StructureSheafAb.lean

**Status: RESOLVED — all 3 sorries closed.**

Iteration 004 prover round, Objective 1 (Phase A steps 2–4 wiring).
File compiles cleanly via `lean_diagnostic_messages` (no errors, no warnings)
and `lake env lean AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/StructureSheafAb.lean` (exit 0).
Axiom hygiene: `lean_verify` on each of the three new declarations returns only
`propext`, `Classical.choice`, `Quot.sound` (the standard Mathlib triple).
**No new `axiom` declarations were introduced or considered.**

## Summary

The plan-agent live-Mathlib probe in `PROGRESS.md` was correct on the nose:
each of the three closures lands in one or two lines, exactly the shape the
probe predicted. No `b80f227` name adjustments were required, no
universe-pinning fights beyond the explicit `.{u}` annotations the scaffold
already carried, and no helper lemmas needed introducing.

## instHasSheafify_Opens_AddCommGrp (L34)

### Attempt 1
- **Approach:** Plan-agent probe verbatim — `inferInstance` after universe
  pinning. Pre-validated by `lean_run_code` on the imports of the file
  before editing.
- **Result:** RESOLVED.
- **Closure body:**

  ```lean
  instance instHasSheafify_Opens_AddCommGrp (X : TopCat.{u}) :
      CategoryTheory.HasSheafify (Opens.grothendieckTopology X)
        AddCommGrpCat.{u} :=
    inferInstance
  ```

- **Key insight:** Mathlib `b80f227` has the full chain
  `HasWeakSheafify` + small-site / concrete-category sheafification API for
  `AddCommGrpCat`-valued sheaves on `Opens.grothendieckTopology X` already
  wired as instances; with the explicit `.{u}` on each category in the
  signature, `inferInstance` discharges the goal.

## instHasExt_Sheaf_Opens_AddCommGrp (L42)

### Attempt 1
- **Approach:** Plan-agent probe verbatim — `CategoryTheory.HasExt.standard _`.
  Pre-validated by `lean_run_code`.
- **Result:** RESOLVED.
- **Closure body:**

  ```lean
  noncomputable instance instHasExt_Sheaf_Opens_AddCommGrp (X : TopCat.{u}) :
      CategoryTheory.HasExt.{u+1}
        (CategoryTheory.Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X)
          AddCommGrpCat.{u}) :=
    CategoryTheory.HasExt.standard _
  ```

- **Key insight:** `HasExt.standard` produces a `HasExt` instance from any
  abelian category. The abelian instance on
  `Sheaf (Opens.gT X) AddCommGrpCat.{u}` is provided automatically by
  Mathlib via `Sheaf.instAbelian`, fed by the
  `instHasSheafify_Opens_AddCommGrp` of the previous step plus the iter-003
  `HasSheafCompose` ingredient. The `HasExt.{u+1}` annotation in the scaffold
  is correct — `HasExt.standard _` unifies its universe with `u+1` because
  the morphism universe of the sheaf category is `u+1` (one higher than the
  underlying-set / `AddCommGrpCat.{u}` universe).

## Scheme.toAbSheaf (L54)

### Attempt 1
- **Approach:** Plan-agent probe verbatim — `(sheafCompose J F).obj C.sheaf`
  with `F` the forget composite. Pre-validated by `lean_run_code` against
  the imports of the file (`AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.SheafCompose`,
  which carries `instHasSheafCompose_forget_CommRing_AddCommGrp`).
- **Result:** RESOLVED.
- **Closure body:**

  ```lean
  noncomputable def toAbSheaf (C : Scheme.{u}) :
      CategoryTheory.Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.toTopCat)
        AddCommGrpCat.{u} :=
    (CategoryTheory.sheafCompose (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.toTopCat)
        (CategoryTheory.forget₂ CommRingCat.{u} RingCat.{u} ⋙
          CategoryTheory.forget₂ RingCat.{u} AddCommGrpCat.{u})).obj C.sheaf
  ```

- **Key insight:** `CategoryTheory.sheafCompose J F` is Mathlib's bundled
  "post-compose with `F`" functor on the sheaf category. Its `obj` map,
  applied to `C.sheaf : Sheaf (Opens.gT X) CommRingCat`, produces the
  desired `Sheaf (Opens.gT X) AddCommGrpCat`. The required
  `(Opens.gT X).HasSheafCompose F` instance is supplied by
  `AlgebraicGeometry.Cohomology.instHasSheafCompose_forget_CommRing_AddCommGrp`
  from `Cohomology/SheafCompose.lean` — typeclass resolution picks it up
  automatically once the universe annotations on the two `forget₂` factors
  match the iter-003 instance verbatim (`CommRingCat.{u} → RingCat.{u} →
  AddCommGrpCat.{u}`).

## Forbidden shortcuts

- No `:= sorry` left.
- No `:= ()` / `:= PUnit` / vacuous-sheaf placeholder.
- No `decide` or `trivial`.
- No new `axiom` declarations.
- No `Discrete PUnit` or constant-`0` shortcuts.

## Mathlib `b80f227` name adjustments

None required. Every Mathlib symbol invoked
(`HasSheafify`, `Opens.grothendieckTopology`, `AddCommGrpCat`, `HasExt`,
`HasExt.standard`, `Sheaf`, `sheafCompose`, `forget₂`, `Scheme.sheaf`,
`CommRingCat`, `RingCat`) lines up with the scaffold's import set
(`AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.SheafCompose`, which itself imports `Mathlib`).

## Sorry count

- Before this round (in this file): 3 (`L34`, `L42`, `L54`).
- After this round (in this file): 0.
- Project-wide impact: 14 → 11 (still pending Objective 2 in
  `Picard/FunctorAb.lean`, the deferred `representable` in `Picard/Functor.lean`,
  and the 9 protected sorries).

## Proposed `\leanok` markers (for the review agent)

In `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_StructureSheafAb.tex`:

- `thm:HasSheafify_Opens_AddCommGrp` — `\leanok` on **both** the statement
  block and the proof block (the proof is `inferInstance`, fully closed,
  no `sorry`).
- `thm:HasExt_Sheaf_Opens_AddCommGrp` — `\leanok` on **both** the statement
  block and the proof block (the proof is `HasExt.standard _`, fully closed).
- `def:Scheme_toAbSheaf` — `\leanok` on the statement block. (It is a
  definition; the body is the term itself, no separate proof block.)

## Next steps

For this file: none. The three Phase A wiring obligations are discharged.
The downstream consumer

```
H^n(C, struct C) := (Scheme.toAbSheaf C).H n
```

is now well-typed (Mathlib's `Sheaf.H` consumes the `HasExt` instance plus
the sheaf, both of which this file now provides). Phase A still has steps 5
(`Module k` structure on `H¹`) and 6 (Serre finiteness) outstanding, but
those are out of scope for iter-004.

## Dead-end warnings (none)

No exploration of failed paths was needed: the plan-agent probe predicted
the closures correctly. Future iterations should reuse this file's
universe-pinning style verbatim when adding further `Sheaf … AddCommGrpCat`
instances on Grothendieck topologies (e.g.\ for the étale topology, if and
when Mathlib closes the `HasWeakSheafify Scheme.etaleTopology AddCommGrpCat`
gap).

1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are either the plan agent, a prover agent, the refactor agent, or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	If instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**. Specifically:
8	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` (local to this project) override Archon's global prompts
9	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` (local to this project) override globally installed plugins
10	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project
11	
12	When in doubt, follow instructions from files inside this project over any external source.
13	
14	## Skills
15	- archon-lean4: installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source) — provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, and other Lean4 commands
16	
17	## Tools
18	- archon-lean-lsp: Lean LSP MCP server (project scope) — use for all Lean LSP operations (search, diagnostics, goal inspection)
19	- archon-informal-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py` (symlinked from Archon) — call external LLMs (OpenAI/Gemini) for informal mathematical reasoning
20	
21	## Key Files & Permissions
22	
23	All state files are in `.archon/`:
24	
25	| File | Plan Agent | Prover Agent | Refactor Agent | Review Agent | User |
26	|------|-----------|-------------|---------------|-------------|------|
27	| `.archon/PROGRESS.md` | read + write | **read only** | read only | read only | read |
28	| `.archon/STRATEGY.md` | **read + write** | do not read | do not read | do not read | read |
29	| `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` | read (then clear) | do not read | do not read | do not read | write |
30	| `.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md` | **write** | do not read | **read** (then execute) | do not read | read |
31	| `.archon/task_pending.md` | read + write | **read only** | read only | read only | read |
32	| `.archon/task_done.md` | read + write | **read only** | read only | read only | read |
33	| `.archon/task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect results) | write (own file only) | write (refactor.md) | read only | read |
34	| `.archon/proof-journal/` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
35	| `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
36	| `archon-protected.yaml` | **read** | **read** | **read + limited write** (file path rename only) | read | **write** |
37	| `.lean` files | do not edit | write (own file only, frozen protected signatures) | **write (all files; protected decls may be moved but not renamed/re-signed)** | do not edit | write (via comments) |
38	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints, structure) | do not edit | do not edit | **write** (markers only: `\leanok`, `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections) | read |
39	
40	## Protected Declarations
41	
42	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Every agent must consult it before editing a `.lean` file. Rules:
43	
44	- **Plan / prover / review agents**: read-only on protected signatures. You may fill proof bodies, but not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
45	- **Refactor agent**: may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (keeping name + signature verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. Refactor agents may never rename, re-type, delete, or re-sign a protected declaration.
46	- The `archon-protected.yaml` file can only be edited by the user, no declaration can be added or removed by any agent. The only allowed modification is updating the file path of an existing protected declaration when its location was changed. 
47	
48	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
49	
50	The blueprint uses two active markers.
51	
52	- `\leanok` — inside a statement block when the declaration is formalized with at least a `sorry`; inside a proof block when the proof is fully closed with no `sorry`.
53	- `\mathlibok` — inside a statement block when the declaration already exists in Mathlib and the Archon side is a re-export/alias; no Archon proof obligation remains.
54	- No marker — the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
55	
56	**The review agent is the sole writer of these markers.** Prover agents (autoformalize / prover / polish) never touch the blueprint chapters — they describe their outcome in `task_results/<file>.md` and the review agent verifies and marks accordingly. The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints, but never markers.
57	
58	## User Interaction
59	
60	Users provide hints in two places:
61	
62	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The plan agent reads this and translates hints into concrete objectives. Provers never read this file.
63	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
64	
65	## Agent Roles
66	
67	### Plan Agent
68	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for your full instructions
69	- Read `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` — incorporate hints, then clear them after acting
70	- Read `.archon/task_results/` — collect prover results, then update `task_pending.md` and `task_done.md`
71	- Read `.archon/task_results/refactor.md` - if refactor agent has run, read their report and adjust your plans accordingly
72	- Write `.archon/PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round
73	- Write `.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md` when structural changes are needed
74	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself
75	
76	### Prover Agent
77	- Read `.archon/PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
78	- Read the stage-specific prompt from `.archon/prompts/`:
79	  - autoformalize → `.archon/prompts/prover-autoformalize.md`
80	  - prover → `.archon/prompts/prover-prover.md`
81	  - polish → `.archon/prompts/prover-polish.md`
82	- Write results to `.archon/task_results/<your_file>.md`
83	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — **never edit another agent's file**
84	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your `.lean` file for file-specific hints
85	- **Do NOT edit blueprint chapters.** Marker updates are the review agent's responsibility. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker.
86	
87	### Refactor Agent
88	- Read `.archon/prompts/refactor.md` for your full instructions
89	- Read `.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md` for the plan agent's directive
90	- Modify any `.lean` file: definitions, signatures, types, imports
91	- Keep all files compiling (insert `sorry` at broken proof sites)
92	- Do NOT fill proofs — that's the prover's job
93	- Write results to `.archon/task_results/refactor.md`
94	
95	### Review Agent
96	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for your full instructions
97	- Read `.archon/proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data
98	- Write session journal to `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (summary.md, milestones.jsonl, recommendations.md)
99	- Update `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` with overall progress
100	- **Update blueprint markers** (`\leanok`, `\mathlibok`) in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` based on verified compilation and the provers' task results
101	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify PROGRESS.md

1	# Prover — Prover Stage
2	
3	You are the prover agent in the proving stage. Your job: fill `sorry` placeholders with complete proofs.
4	
5	## Workflow
6	
7	1. Read `PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
8	2. Read `task_pending.md` for context on your assigned file — prior attempts, dead ends, relevant lemmas
9	3. Check your `.lean` file for `/- USER: ... -/` comments — these are file-specific hints from the user
10	4. Before writing Lean code, you **MUST** consult the relevant blueprint chapter. Blueprints contain mathematical proof sketches; your formal proof must align with them. When stuck, re-reading the blueprint is often the fastest path forward.
11	5. Replace `sorry` with Lean proofs, pushing as far as possible
12	6. **Always save partial progress in the code.** If you cannot fully prove a sorry, replace it with your best attempt — commented-out proof steps, helper lemmas, partial `by` blocks with remaining `sorry` at the stuck point. The file must still compile (use scoped `sorry` for the stuck parts), but your work must be visible in the code for the next agent to continue from. NEVER revert to the original bare `sorry` — that wastes all your work.
13	7. Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md` — what you tried, what worked, what's stuck, next steps
14	
15	**Write permissions**: You may only write to your assigned `.lean` file(s) and your `task_results/<file>.md`. Do NOT edit `PROGRESS.md`, `task_pending.md`, `task_done.md`, or other agents' files.
16	
17	## Protected declarations
18	
19	Before editing, read `archon-protected.yaml` at the project root. Declarations listed for your file have **frozen signatures** — you may fill their proof bodies but must not rename them, change their types, reorder their arguments, or weaken their hypotheses. Only the mathematician is allowed to edit protected signatures. 
20	
21	## Avoid Early Termination
22	
23	- Do not abandon a proof prematurely
24	- Do not delegate to the next iteration or another prover if it can be solved with more effort
25	- Many complex problems require thousands of lines of Lean code
26	- Do not stop and leave a sorry simply because the proof is long
27	- Task difficulty is NOT a valid reason to leave `sorry` placeholders
28	- Only modify the proof corresponding to the task; leave other proofs/declarations untouched
29	- **Decomposition**: Act like a mathematician — systematically break the proof into smaller sub-problems (following the blueprint's lemma structure if available: L1, L2, L3, …) and solve each one individually until the entire goal is closed
30	
31	## Task Completion Criteria
32	
33	Your task is NOT complete until ALL of:
34	1. Every `sorry` has been replaced with a complete proof
35	2. Zero axioms introduced
36	3. The file compiles successfully with no errors
37	
38	If you encounter obstacles:
39	- Break the problem into smaller subgoals
40	- Search for relevant Mathlib lemmas more thoroughly
41	- Prove missing helper lemmas yourself
42	- Try alternative proof strategies
43	- Consult the informal proof / blueprint for guidance
44	- Use Web Search to find paper proofs when Mathlib lacks a theorem
45	
46	### When infrastructure is missing or the current route is too hard
47	
48	Do NOT just report "Mathlib lacks X" and stop. Before giving up on a sorry, you must try to find an alternative yourself:
49	
50	1. **Use the informal agent** (`.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py`) — ask: "Prove [goal] without using [missing infrastructure], only using tools available in Lean 4 Mathlib." Even an imperfect sketch is valuable.
51	2. **Try the alternative** — if the informal agent gives you a route, attempt to formalize it.
52	3. **If you still can't solve it**, save what you learned for the plan agent:
53	   - Write the informal agent's alternative proof sketch to `informal/<theorem_name>.md`
54	   - In your `task_results/<file>.md`, record: what you tried, why it failed, AND the alternative route you found (even if unverified). This gives the plan agent concrete material to work with — not just "it's hard."
55	   - A prover that reports "I couldn't prove X, but here's an alternative approach via Y that might work because Z" is far more useful than one that just says "infrastructure missing."
56	
57	## Proof Style
58	
59	- **Never modify working proofs** — if a declaration has no `sorry` and compiles, do not touch its proof body
60	- Keep edits minimal: do not delete comments or change labels
61	- Do not add unrelated declarations
62	- **Intermediate helper lemmas you introduced** may be modified if they turn out to be incorrect or need adjustment.
63	- Add concise, informative comments above helper lemmas to make later reuse easy
64	
65	## Search Protocol
66	
67	Follow the search tool priority and query guidance in the lean4 skill reference (`references/lean-lsp-tools-api.md`). Key points:
68	
69	1. `lean_local_search` first
70	2. `lean_leansearch` for semantic search — **describe the mathematical content**, not just the name
71	3. `lean_loogle` for simple type patterns only
72	4. Never use local file search (find, grep) to locate Mathlib theorems
73	
74	## Missing Lemmas & Impossibility
75	
76	Follow the lean4 skill reference (`references/sorry-filling.md`) for:
77	- **When Mathlib lacks a theorem**: bypass or implement yourself. Web Search for published papers. Never leave a `sorry` just because Mathlib doesn't have it.
78	- **Distinguish impossibility from difficulty**: technical difficulty → keep trying. Mathematical impossibility → immediately backtrack and document why.
79	
80	## Logging
81	
82	Write your results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`. Use the file name from your assigned `.lean` file (e.g., if you own `Algebra/WLocal.lean`, write to `task_results/Algebra_WLocal.lean.md`).
83	
84	**Format:**
85	
86	```markdown
87	# Algebra/WLocal.lean
88	
89	## wLocal_iff (line 45)
90	### Attempt 1
91	- **Approach:** Direct case split on maximal ideals
92	- **Result:** FAILED — needed IsLocalRing instance not available
93	- **Dead end:** Do not try direct case split without IsLocalRing
94	
95	### Attempt 2
96	- **Approach:** Use Stacks 0A31, characterize via bijection on spectra
97	- **Result:** RESOLVED
98	- **Key insight:** Mathlib's PrimeSpectrum.comap_injective bridges the gap
99	
100	## helper_bijective (line 78)
101	### Attempt 1
102	- **Approach:** Function.Bijective.comp
103	- **Result:** IN PROGRESS — stuck on surjectivity
104	- **Next step:** Try PrimeSpectrum.range_comap_of_surjective
105	- **Relevant lemmas found:** PrimeSpectrum.comap_surjective
106	```
107	
108	**Rules:**
109	1. One section per theorem/lemma in your file
110	2. Each attempt records: approach, result (RESOLVED / FAILED / IN PROGRESS), dead-end warnings or next steps
111	3. Log negative search results (e.g., "Searched 'projective module infinite rank' — nothing in Mathlib")
112	4. The plan agent collects these files and merges them into `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`
113	
114	**Read-only references:** Read `task_pending.md` for prior context on your file. Read `task_done.md` if the current problem resembles a completed one. Do not write to either.
115	
116	## Summary Pipeline
117	
118	1. Read `task_pending.md` and `task_done.md` for context from prior sessions
119	2. Read the informal proof / blueprint to understand the proof strategy and lemma decomposition
120	3. Introduce helper lemmas (matching the blueprint's structure) in the `.lean` file
121	4. Replace `sorry` placeholders with complete proofs, ensuring the file compiles without errors
122	5. Do not modify protected declarations listed in `archon-protected.yaml`.
123	6. Use Mathlib theorems when possible. Use Web Search when Mathlib lacks referenced results
124	7. Rely on Lean LSP for diagnostics; use `lake env lean <file>` sparingly for final checks
125	8. Log all explorations in `task_results/<your_file>.md`
126	
127	## End-of-session handoff
128	
129	Before you stop (or when you are done with this round of work):
130	
131	1. Write to `task_results/<your_file>.md` with:
132	   - Current result (IN PROGRESS / FAILED) and what you tried
133	   - Any Mathlib lemmas you discovered that are relevant
134	   - Concrete next step for the next session
135	   - Dead-end warnings for approaches that won't work
136	2. Save all file changes (ensure compilation passes, using scoped `sorry` if needed)

6	import Mathlib
7	
8	/-!
9	# Sheaf condition along the structure-sheaf forget composite
10	
11	Phase A step 1 (per `STRATEGY.md`): the `HasSheafCompose` instance for the
12	forget composite `CommRingCat ⥤ RingCat ⥤ AddCommGrpCat` on the Grothendieck
13	topology of opens of an arbitrary topological space. This is the gateway to
14	computing `H¹(C, O_C)` as an abelian group (and ultimately as a `k`-vector
15	space for the genus).
16	
17	See `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_SheafCompose.tex`.
18	
19	## Status (iteration 004 — refactor scaffold)
20	
21	This file is a scaffold. The single instance below is `sorry`. The iter-005
22	prover is responsible for filling it (Phase A step 1 of `STRATEGY.md`).
23	-/
24	
25	set_option autoImplicit false
26	
27	universe u
28	
29	open CategoryTheory Limits
30	
31	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Cohomology
32	
33	/-- The forget composite `CommRingCat ⥤ RingCat ⥤ AddCommGrpCat` preserves the
34	sheaf condition on the Grothendieck topology of opens of any topological space.
35	Equivalently: a sheaf of commutative rings on a topological space is in
36	particular a sheaf of abelian groups (post-composing with the forgetful is
37	sound). -/
38	instance instHasSheafCompose_forget_CommRing_AddCommGrp (X : TopCat.{u}) :
39	    (Opens.grothendieckTopology X).HasSheafCompose
40	      (CategoryTheory.forget₂ CommRingCat.{u} RingCat.{u} ⋙
41	       CategoryTheory.forget₂ RingCat.{u} AddCommGrpCat.{u}) :=
42	  haveI : PreservesLimitsOfSize.{u, u}
43	      (CategoryTheory.forget₂ CommRingCat.{u} RingCat.{u} ⋙
44	        CategoryTheory.forget₂ RingCat.{u} AddCommGrpCat.{u}) :=
45	    Limits.comp_preservesLimits _ _
46	  CategoryTheory.hasSheafCompose_of_preservesLimitsOfSize _
47	
48	end AlgebraicGeometry.Cohomology

## `Scheme.toAbSheaf` (L56)

### Attempt 1
- **Approach:** Use `CategoryTheory.sheafCompose` with the iter-003 forget-composite `HasSheafCompose` instance, applied to `C.sheaf : Sheaf (Opens.gT C.toTopCat) CommRingCat`.
- **Result:** RESOLVED — Mathlib's `sheafCompose J F` is the bundled "post-compose with `F`" functor on the sheaf category; its `obj` map applied to `C.sheaf` gives the desired `Sheaf (Opens.gT C.toTopCat) AddCommGrpCat`. The forget-composite `HasSheafCompose` instance from `Cohomology/SheafCompose.lean` (iter-003) is picked up automatically by typeclass resolution.
- **Closure body:**
  ```lean
  noncomputable def toAbSheaf (C : Scheme.{u}) :
      CategoryTheory.Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.toTopCat)
        AddCommGrpCat.{u} :=
    (CategoryTheory.sheafCompose (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.toTopCat)
      (CategoryTheory.forget₂ CommRingCat.{u} RingCat.{u} ⋙
        CategoryTheory.forget₂ RingCat.{u} AddCommGrpCat.{u})).obj C.sheaf
  ```
- **Axioms:** `propext`, `Classical.choice`, `Quot.sound` (standard).
- **Key insight:** Universe pinning on the two `forget₂` factors matches the iter-003 `HasSheafCompose` instance verbatim, so typeclass resolution finds the instance on the nose without any explicit instance argument.

## Independent verification

- `lean_run_code` with `import Mathlib` ran the three closures as `example`s in isolation: success with zero diagnostics. (Sanity check before editing the file.)
- Post-edit `lean_diagnostic_messages` on the file: `success=true`, zero items (no errors, no warnings — sorries gone).
- Per-decl `lean_verify` for each of the three names: no `sorryAx`, no project-introduced axiom; only `propext` / `Classical.choice` / `Quot.sound`.
- `grep -n 'sorry\|axiom' …/StructureSheafAb.lean`: only one match, on a doc-comment line (no semantic sorry/axiom).

## Side notes

- I refreshed the file's status block (`## Status (iteration 004 — prover round closed)`) so the doc comment no longer claims this is a scaffold. No declaration name, signature, or comment label was renamed; the doc string of each declaration is preserved verbatim.
- No protected declaration touched (none of the three are listed in `archon-protected.yaml`).
- No new `axiom` declarations introduced (and none were considered).
- No new imports added; `AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.SheafCompose` (which itself imports `Mathlib`) is sufficient.
- No name-adjustment was needed against Mathlib `b80f227`. The plan-agent probe predicted the exact API shapes used.

## Forbidden shortcuts considered

- `Discrete PUnit` / vacuous-sheaf placeholder: NOT used.
- `decide` / `trivial`: NOT used.
- New `axiom` declarations: NOT introduced (and not considered).
- Bare `sorry` left in the file: NONE.

## Proposed `\leanok` markers (for the review agent)

The blueprint chapter `Cohomology_StructureSheafAb.tex` carries three blocks for which `\leanok` is now justified:

- `thm:HasSheafify_Opens_AddCommGrp` — **statement** `\leanok` and **proof** `\leanok` (proof is `inferInstance`, a single line, fully closed; no `sorry`).
- `thm:HasExt_Sheaf_Opens_AddCommGrp` — **statement** `\leanok` and **proof** `\leanok` (proof is `HasExt.standard _`, a single line, fully closed; no `sorry`).
- `def:Scheme_toAbSheaf` — **statement** `\leanok` (definition is fully closed; no `sorry`). No separate proof block.

All three line up with the blueprint's `\lean{...}` macros (`AlgebraicGeometry.Cohomology.instHasSheafify_Opens_AddCommGrp`, `AlgebraicGeometry.Cohomology.instHasExt_Sheaf_Opens_AddCommGrp`, `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.toAbSheaf`) verbatim.

## Anticipated downstream impact

After this round, `(Scheme.toAbSheaf C).H n` is well-typed (Mathlib's `Sheaf.H` API requires `HasExt` on the sheaf category, which the new instance provides). The remaining Phase A blockers for `genus` are step 5 (`Module k` structure on `H¹`) and step 6 (Serre finiteness); both require new mathematical content and are out of scope for this round.

## Next session

Nothing pending on this file — all three sorries are closed honestly. The plan agent's iter-005 round will pick up Phase A steps 5/6 (deferred mathematical content) or any cross-cutting consolidation.

1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are either the plan agent, a prover agent, the refactor agent, or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	If instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**. Specifically:
8	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` (local to this project) override Archon's global prompts
9	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` (local to this project) override globally installed plugins
10	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project
11	
12	When in doubt, follow instructions from files inside this project over any external source.
13	
14	## Skills
15	- archon-lean4: installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source) — provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, and other Lean4 commands
16	
17	## Tools
18	- archon-lean-lsp: Lean LSP MCP server (project scope) — use for all Lean LSP operations (search, diagnostics, goal inspection)
19	- archon-informal-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py` (symlinked from Archon) — call external LLMs (OpenAI/Gemini) for informal mathematical reasoning
20	
21	## Key Files & Permissions
22	
23	All state files are in `.archon/`:
24	
25	| File | Plan Agent | Prover Agent | Refactor Agent | Review Agent | User |
26	|------|-----------|-------------|---------------|-------------|------|
27	| `.archon/PROGRESS.md` | read + write | **read only** | read only | read only | read |
28	| `.archon/STRATEGY.md` | **read + write** | do not read | do not read | do not read | read |
29	| `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` | read (then clear) | do not read | do not read | do not read | write |
30	| `.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md` | **write** | do not read | **read** (then execute) | do not read | read |
31	| `.archon/task_pending.md` | read + write | **read only** | read only | read only | read |
32	| `.archon/task_done.md` | read + write | **read only** | read only | read only | read |
33	| `.archon/task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect results) | write (own file only) | write (refactor.md) | read only | read |
34	| `.archon/proof-journal/` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
35	| `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
36	| `archon-protected.yaml` | **read** | **read** | **read + limited write** (file path rename only) | read | **write** |
37	| `.lean` files | do not edit | write (own file only, frozen protected signatures) | **write (all files; protected decls may be moved but not renamed/re-signed)** | do not edit | write (via comments) |
38	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints, structure) | do not edit | do not edit | **write** (markers only: `\leanok`, `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections) | read |
39	
40	## Protected Declarations
41	
42	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Every agent must consult it before editing a `.lean` file. Rules:
43	
44	- **Plan / prover / review agents**: read-only on protected signatures. You may fill proof bodies, but not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
45	- **Refactor agent**: may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (keeping name + signature verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. Refactor agents may never rename, re-type, delete, or re-sign a protected declaration.
46	- The `archon-protected.yaml` file can only be edited by the user, no declaration can be added or removed by any agent. The only allowed modification is updating the file path of an existing protected declaration when its location was changed. 
47	
48	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
49	
50	The blueprint uses two active markers.
51	
52	- `\leanok` — inside a statement block when the declaration is formalized with at least a `sorry`; inside a proof block when the proof is fully closed with no `sorry`.
53	- `\mathlibok` — inside a statement block when the declaration already exists in Mathlib and the Archon side is a re-export/alias; no Archon proof obligation remains.
54	- No marker — the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
55	
56	**The review agent is the sole writer of these markers.** Prover agents (autoformalize / prover / polish) never touch the blueprint chapters — they describe their outcome in `task_results/<file>.md` and the review agent verifies and marks accordingly. The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints, but never markers.
57	
58	## User Interaction
59	
60	Users provide hints in two places:
61	
62	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The plan agent reads this and translates hints into concrete objectives. Provers never read this file.
63	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
64	
65	## Agent Roles
66	
67	### Plan Agent
68	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for your full instructions
69	- Read `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` — incorporate hints, then clear them after acting
70	- Read `.archon/task_results/` — collect prover results, then update `task_pending.md` and `task_done.md`
71	- Read `.archon/task_results/refactor.md` - if refactor agent has run, read their report and adjust your plans accordingly
72	- Write `.archon/PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round
73	- Write `.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md` when structural changes are needed
74	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` (except for marker updates) and `\lean{...}` hints for the provers
75	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself
76	
77	### Prover Agent
78	- Read `.archon/PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
79	- Read the stage-specific prompt from `.archon/prompts/`:
80	  - autoformalize → `.archon/prompts/prover-autoformalize.md`
81	  - prover → `.archon/prompts/prover-prover.md`
82	  - polish → `.archon/prompts/prover-polish.md`
83	- Write results to `.archon/task_results/<your_file>.md`
84	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — **never edit another agent's file**
85	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your `.lean` file for file-specific hints
86	- **Do NOT edit blueprint chapters.** Marker updates are the review agent's responsibility. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker.
87	
88	### Refactor Agent
89	- Read `.archon/prompts/refactor.md` for your full instructions
90	- Read `.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md` for the plan agent's directive
91	- Modify any `.lean` file: definitions, signatures, types, imports
92	- Keep all files compiling (insert `sorry` at broken proof sites)
93	- Do NOT fill proofs — that's the prover's job
94	- Write results to `.archon/task_results/refactor.md`
95	
96	### Review Agent
97	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for your full instructions
98	- Read `.archon/proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data
99	- Write session journal to `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (summary.md, milestones.jsonl, recommendations.md)
100	- Update `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` with overall progress
101	- **Update blueprint markers** (`\leanok`, `\mathlibok`) in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` based on verified compilation and the provers' task results
102	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify PROGRESS.md
